name: Autopilot Leasing
description_short: Brooklyn-based auto leasing broker offering vehicle leasing, financing, and purchases with concierge door-to-door delivery across New York metro.
description_long:
Autopilot Leasing is a New York-based auto leasing broker and dealer that positions itself as a concierge alternative to traditional car dealerships. Operating primarily in the New York metro area with a presence in Brooklyn, the company's stated mission is to streamline the entire vehicle acquisition process from initial inquiry through final delivery. No founding date is publicly disclosed, and the company does not hold certifications such as CDFI, HUD, or NFCC approval — categories not applicable to an auto leasing operation. Their website is autopilotleasing.com, though as of March 2026, at least one site page returned a suspended account notice, suggesting a small-scale or still-evolving operation.

Autopilot Leasing offers three core services: vehicle leasing, vehicle financing, and vehicle purchases. Their leasing product emphasizes flexible terms, low monthly payments, and access to the latest models across all major car brands. The financing arm targets customers with varying credit backgrounds, advertising quick approvals and flexible payment schedules — positioning that indicates they work with a broad credit spectrum rather than prime-only borrowers. Both new and pre-owned vehicle purchases are available. A standout feature is door-to-door delivery, in which the completed vehicle is brought directly to the customer's home or office. No specific APR ranges, loan amounts, or monthly payment figures are published; all pricing appears to be deal-by-deal and requires direct contact.

The company's defining differentiator is its concierge model: customers receive personalized guidance through every step rather than navigating a conventional dealership experience. Autopilot explicitly claims transparent, upfront pricing with no hidden fees — a meaningful promise if upheld in practice. As of March 2026, the company holds a 5.0-out-of-5 Google rating from 93 reviewers, an unusually high satisfaction score for a consumer services business. At least one review reference mentions delivery completed in under 24 hours, suggesting genuine operational efficiency behind the concierge marketing.

The main limitation is the near-complete absence of verifiable public information. No pricing, APR disclosures, or fee structures are published, making cost comparisons impossible without a direct quote. No BBB profile was located, leaving no independent record of complaint handling or dispute resolution. The suspended-account notice on the privacy policy page raises additional questions about operational stability or website maintenance. Consumers should verify current operational status directly, request a full breakdown of all fees before signing any lease or financing agreement, and compare total cost of ownership against competing brokers and direct dealership offers.
pros: 5.0/5 Google rating from 93 reviewers — one of the strongest satisfaction scores in its category
Door-to-door vehicle delivery to home or office eliminates dealership visits entirely
At least one customer review references delivery completed in under 24 hours
Claims transparent, upfront pricing with no hidden fees — directly addresses a common dealership pain point
Vehicle financing available across a range of credit situations, not limited to prime borrowers
Covers all major car brands, offering broad inventory flexibility
Concierge-style personalized support throughout the leasing or purchase process
cons: No pricing, APR ranges, or fee structures published — every deal requires a direct quote, making comparison-shopping difficult
Website privacy policy page returned a 'suspended account' notice as of March 2026, raising questions about operational scale or stability
No BBB profile found — no independent third-party record of complaint history or dispute resolution
Limited publicly verifiable information: founding year, licensing details, staff size, and physical address not indexed
Appears to serve the New York metro area only — no indication of service beyond this region
best_for: New York metro area residents who want to lease or buy a car without setting foot in a dealership
Buyers with mixed or non-prime credit histories seeking flexible vehicle financing with quick approvals
Consumers who prioritize convenience and want a vehicle delivered directly to their home or office
Individuals who find traditional dealership negotiations stressful and prefer a managed, concierge process
diagnosis: Autopilot Leasing is best suited for New York-area consumers who want a streamlined, dealership-free car leasing or financing experience, particularly those who value home delivery and personalized support over the lowest possible sticker price. The 5.0-star Google rating from 93 reviews is a genuine positive signal, but the absence of published pricing, no BBB profile, and a partially suspended website mean prospective customers should verify current operational status and request a complete fee breakdown before committing to any agreement.
services: Car leasing with flexible terms and low monthly payments
Vehicle financing for a range of credit profiles
Multi-brand vehicle sourcing across major manufacturers
